prerequisites: A RepairShopr account with administrator access to the More > Admin > API - API Tokens area, where API tokens are created and managed.

Your RepairShopr account subdomain, which is the prefix of your account URL (the <subdomain> in https://<subdomain>.repairshopr.com). This is required for the connector's subdomain config field and forms the API base URL https://<subdomain>.repairshopr.com/api/v1/.
An API token (key) generated from the API Tokens admin page, copied and saved at creation time because the key is not shown again afterward.

authMethods.name: API key (API token)
authMethods.summary: RepairShopr authenticates API requests with an API token. In the admin UI go to More > Admin > API - API Tokens and click New Token, where you can give it a name, set an optional expiration date, and assign granular permissions, then create it. Copy the generated key immediately, as it cannot be viewed again. Provide this value in the connector's api_key secret field.

setupSteps.title: Find your account subdomain
setupSteps.body: Sign in to RepairShopr and note the subdomain in your account URL: the <subdomain> portion of https://<subdomain>.repairshopr.com. Enter this exact value in the connector's subdomain config field. It is used to build the API base URL https://<subdomain>.repairshopr.com/api/v1/.

setupSteps.title: Create an API token
setupSteps.body: As an administrator, navigate to More > Admin > API - API Tokens and click New Token in the upper right. Assign a name, optionally set an expiration date, and select the permissions the connector needs to read your data. Click create to generate the token.

setupSteps.title: Copy and store the token
setupSteps.body: Copy the generated key and save it somewhere secure right away. RepairShopr will not display the key again after creation.

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials to the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the copied token in the connector's api_key field and the account subdomain in the subdomain field. Both fields are required.

sources.title: RepairShopr REST API - Build custom extensions/apps/addons (knowledge base)
Feature Release - API Updates: New Token System with Granular Permissions and a New Authentication Method
